diagrams.title: Linear Search Algorithm Visualization
diagrams.description: Shows the step-by-step process of linear search where each element is checked sequentially until the target is found or the array ends. Visualizes the linear progression through the array with current position highlighted.
elements: Array of unsorted elements with indices
diagrams.purpose: Explains how linear search works by iterating through elements one by one, demonstrating the O(n) time complexity concept through visual progression

diagrams.title: Binary Search Algorithm Visualization
diagrams.description: Demonstrates the divide-and-conquer approach of binary search on a sorted array. Shows how the search space halves with each comparison by identifying the middle element and determining which half contains the target.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the binary search mechanism showing how the search space is reduced by half each iteration, visualizing the O(log n) time complexity

diagrams.title: Linear vs Binary Search Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing the fundamental difference between linear and binary search approaches. Demonstrates how linear search checks elements sequentially while binary search jumps to middle elements and narrows the range.
diagrams.purpose: Compares both algorithms to help students understand when to use each approach, reinforcing the requirement of sorted data for binary search and the trade-offs in time complexity
